old_claim.note: Global Positioning System (GPS) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The scoped capability is specifically GPS-based fleet navigation, tracking, routing, and dispatch, so GPS positioning is constitutive rather than optional.

source_shape.source_locator: Commercial Applications and Productivity Enhancement sections: identifies GPS receivers tied to electronic maps as the basis for directions, vehicle-fleet tracking and management, commercial routing, dispatch, and delivery-efficiency gains.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The federal report documents commercial vehicle navigation and fleet-management functions as applications produced by GPS positioning integrated with digital maps.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Because this node is now explicitly bounded to GPS-guided fleet operations, removing GPS would remove the differentiating positioning method rather than merely substitute another implementation.

ontology_before: GPS was treated as one optional enabling route for a broadly named logistics capability.
ontology_after: GPS is a required constituent of the explicitly scoped GPS-guided fleet logistics capability.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-gps-navstar-logistics-boundary: gps remains required for gps_guided_logistics.
would_reject_if: The node is broadened to include non-GPS fleet dispatch or generic route optimization as equivalent instances of the same scoped capability.
short_reason: GPS positioning is constitutive of a node explicitly scoped to GPS-guided fleet logistics.
